consumes.artifact: evals/scenarios/ (new scenarios covering each skill hook)

produces.artifact: Modified SKILL.md files with finish --if-last calls at the correct step
New eval scenarios for sdd-downstream-contract skill hooks (naming: evals/scenarios/sdd-downstream-contract-*.md)
Updated Red Flags / Common Rationalizations tables that close skip-finish loopholes

requirement.title: arc-finishing-epic Step 4 Option 1 calls finish --if-last
requirement.description: skills/arc-finishing-epic/SKILL.md Step 4 Option 1 (merge locally) MUST be updated so that after the cleanup call on line 103 it invokes arcforge finish --spec-id <spec-id> --if-last. This is the single hook that makes Convention C automatic for the most common per-epic-terminus path (arc-implementing, arc-agent-driven, arc- looping, arc-dispatching-parallel all route through here).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given skills/arc-finishing-epic/SKILL.md after implementation, When Step 4 Option 1 (Merge Locally) is read top-to-bottom, Then immediately after the finish-epic.js cleanup invocation currently at line 103 there MUST appear a line invoking arcforge finish --spec-id <spec-id> --if-last (or an equivalent finish-epic.js wrapper that forwards to the same CLI). design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.1

Given an agent session that has just merged the final epic of a sprint via arc-finishing-epic Step 4 Option 1, When the session completes, Then specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST have been removed and a chore(specs): finish <spec-id> sprint commit MUST be present on the current branch, evidencing that the skill hook fired. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.1 (end-to-end)

Given an agent session that merges a non-final epic, When the session completes, Then the --if-last call MUST have exited zero with no-op output and specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST still exist, so that intermediate epic merges do not accidentally delete planner output. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 2 (conditional)

requirement.title: arc-finishing-epic Step 4 Option 2 (PR) must not call finish
requirement.description: skills/arc-finishing-epic/SKILL.md Step 4 Option 2 (Create PR) MUST NOT include any arcforge finish call. PRs land on the remote after human / CI review; local arcforge finish would fail its "all branches fully merged" guard. Users running PR-only sprints invoke arcforge finish manually after all PRs merge. The skill MUST document this in prose so the implementer does not insert finish by symmetry with Option 1.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given skills/arc-finishing-epic/SKILL.md after implementation, When Step 4 Option 2 is read, Then no line in that option MUST invoke arcforge finish (or a wrapper that forwards to it) under any conditional. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.1 (PR carve-out)

Given the same SKILL.md, When the reader reaches the end of Step 4 Option 2, Then prose MUST explain why PR-path does not auto-finish (guard mismatch, remote merge timing), and MUST direct the user to run arcforge finish manually once all PRs merge. design.md §Scope — Excludes (PR-based sprint-end)

requirement.title: arc-dispatching-teammates wrap-up calls finish --if-last
requirement.description: skills/arc-dispatching-teammates/SKILL.md (Step 8b in the body or references/wrap-up-sequence.md, whichever is the authoritative location for the wrap-up prescription) MUST be updated so that after the batch arcforge cleanup call it invokes arcforge finish --spec-id <spec-id> --if-last. The lead session has the global view; this hook covers the multi-teammate dispatch scenario where per-teammate arc-finishing-epic cannot reliably detect "last epic" because teammates run in parallel.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given the authoritative wrap-up location in arc-dispatching- teammates after implementation, When Step 8 (wrap-up) is read top-to-bottom, Then immediately after the batch cleanup call there MUST appear a line invoking arcforge finish --spec-id <spec-id> --if-last. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.2 (Q4-2=b)

Given a lead session that dispatched multiple teammates, all of whom merged their final-sprint epics, When the lead session runs Step 8 wrap-up, Then specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST be removed and the finish commit MUST be present on the lead's branch, evidencing that the lead-side hook fired rather than any individual teammate. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 2 (lead detects last)

requirement.title: arc-coordinating command table lists finish
requirement.description: skills/arc-coordinating/SKILL.md command table MUST add a row for finish with the description "End a sprint (delete dag.yaml + epics/ when all epics completed)" and example invocation arcforge finish --spec-id <id>. This makes the new subcommand discoverable through the same routing surface users already use to learn cleanup, expand, merge, sync, and status.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given skills/arc-coordinating/SKILL.md after implementation, When the command table near line 32 is read, Then a finish row MUST be present with the description noted above and an example arcforge finish --spec-id <id>. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.3

Given the same SKILL.md, When the full document is read for consistency, Then no claim MUST remain that cleanup removes dag.yaml or epics/ (earlier prose that conflated the two scopes MUST be updated so cleanup and finish are clearly separated). design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 3

requirement.title: Skill Red Flags close skip-finish rationalizations
requirement.description: Each of the three modified SKILL.md files (arc-finishing-epic, arc-dispatching-teammates, arc-coordinating) MUST include at least one Red Flag entry (or Common Rationalizations row where that structure is used) that explicitly closes the loophole of skipping finish when it is the last epic. Phrasings MUST name concrete temptations ("This is the last epic but I will just git rm manually" / "finish seems redundant with cleanup"), not vague prohibitions.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given each of the three modified SKILL.md files, When its Red Flags / Common Rationalizations section is read, Then at least one entry MUST explicitly warn against manually performing git rm of dag.yaml / epics in place of calling finish, and MUST state that Convention C is the single sanctioned terminus path. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.4

Given an adversarial eval scenario that presents a last-epic merge and tempts the skill to skip finish (with a plausible-sounding rationalization), When the skill runs with the updated Red Flags in place, Then the agent MUST either invoke arcforge finish --if-last or surface the temptation and refuse it, and MUST NOT manually git rm dag.yaml / epics. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R2.4 (adversarial eval)

requirement.title: Skill-layer changes follow arc-writing-skills TDD-for-docs
requirement.description: The implementation of fr-sh-001 through fr-sh-005 MUST proceed under arc-writing-skills discipline: RED (eval scenarios fail without the skill edits) → GREEN (skill edits land, scenarios pass) → REFACTOR (Red Flags / Rationalizations added to close remaining loopholes observed during REFACTOR). Each of the three skills modified MUST have at least one eval scenario under evals/scenarios/sdd-downstream-contract-*.md whose RED baseline is recorded and whose GREEN pass is verified in the implementing PR.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given the sdd-downstream-contract implementation PR, When the PR description and eval outputs are reviewed, Then at least three eval scenarios MUST be present (one per modified skill) each with a RED baseline evidencing pre-change failure and a GREEN result evidencing post-change success. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 3 discipline

Given the three modified SKILL.md files, When tests/skills/ pytest runs against them, Then every existing validator MUST continue to pass (no frontmatter regressions, no missing required sections, no word- count tier violations beyond the established limits). design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 3 discipline
